events.fires_on: About to invoke a tool (Bash/Edit/Write/etc.) — before side effects

STRONG.semantics: Tool call refused. Harness returns non-zero exit from hook (Claude Code: exit 2; OpenCode: throw; Codex: dict {block:true}).

MEDIUM.semantics: Marker file written at canonical path; warning string surfaced via side-band log; tool call proceeds. Catches lifecycle-gated checks but cannot block real-time.

WEAK.semantics: Warning string injected into next model turn's system prompt (or harness equivalent). No programmatic effect this turn.

events.fires_on: Tool call completed; result available — before model sees it

STRONG.semantics: Result mutation/observation before model sees it. Harness returns modified result.

MEDIUM.semantics: Async log only; result delivered unchanged.

WEAK.semantics: This event is not available at WEAK tier; declarations of WEAK on tool_result fail M4 capability key closure.

events.fires_on: Agent about to end a turn

STRONG.semantics: Stop refused until marker condition met. Harness re-enters the agent loop.

MEDIUM.semantics: Warning logged; stop proceeds. Useful for post-hoc lifecycle audit.

WEAK.semantics: stop at WEAK is not meaningful — by definition the harness has already decided to stop and there's no next turn to inject into.

STRONG.semantics: Context (string/JSON) appended to system prompt; presence verifiable in transcript.

MEDIUM.semantics: Best-effort inject; harness may or may not surface (no verification path).

WEAK.semantics: Inject via static rules/config file deployed at install time (Windsurf .windsurf/rules/). No dynamic per-session content. (Cursor is NOT WEAK — see F43 correction.)

STRONG.semantics: Hook fires deterministically; can write final-state artifacts (handoff files, session summary episodes).

MEDIUM.semantics: Best-effort fire; artifact may be missing on abnormal termination.
